mentions.note: Terrific quarter with 22% iPhone growth, America strength, first China growth in ages; bearish chip-cost thesis overblown; Tim Cook unusually bullish suggesting margin confidence; supply-chain mastery, customer power, and carrier subsidies protect pricing; finished +$120 despite midday weakness.

Tremendous quarter with strong U.S. and shocking China recovery; iPhone revenue +22%; finished up $1.20 despite bearish sell-off on parts shortage fears; Cramer confident supply chain mastery and pricing power will overcome.
Reports this week with very good quarter expected; like the dividend but Astroenica has better portfolio at this moment.

Confusing down-move despite solid earnings beat and guidance raise; market convinced payroll processors doomed by AI despite CFO seeing no discernable impact; fundamentals fine but sentiment terrible.

Down 1.5% on what looked like solid beat and raise; Wall Street convinced payroll processors doomed by AI; CFO says not seeing meaningful AI impact; market dismisses reassurance; broader job slowdown adding pressure; now up 25% from highs but 'house of pain' for now.
Buy now pay later kingpin will put up fantastic quarter; CEO Max Levchin; should be bought ahead of quarter; bears will be put on run.
Pattern has emerged where terrific numbers still result in stock decline; semiconductor sector headwind despite fundamental strength.
Very strong biotech pipeline seen at JP Morgan conference; good earnings plus potential weight-loss drug news could send Dow stock higher.
Controversial stock; formerly roared on earnings but now gets faded on strength and sells off on weakness; owns for Charitable Trust but has not been good returner; let earnings speak.
Cancer franchise turned out to be lot stronger than thought; hold position; better portfolio than Abby at this moment despite higher yield on competitor.
Cancer franchise turned out stronger than expected; superior portfolio vs. AbbVie; hold position for continued upside.
Too risky; impossible to know what asset manager owns inside; historically dangerous to own when you don't know holdings.
Very well-run company with stock down so low; 6.75% yield covered despite massive earnings cuts; CEO Rob Cats doing good job; buy here.
Stock so low it might bounce even if quarter isn't spectacular; valuations attractive after pullback.

Logistics company delivered solid quarter and up 5% but sales declined 6.5% YoY; benefits from market-share gains, not freight recovery; weak ocean and truckload pricing; up 130% from crisis lows despite weak freight demand.
Popped 5% on solid quarter; stock rallied 130% from liberation-day lows; benefiting from market share gains and self-help despite weak freight market with sales down 6.5% YoY; still terrific stock.
Too risky; if you don't know what asset manager owns you don't want to buy; can't find out what it's got inside.
Stock so low it might bounce even if quarter isn't spectacular; may not matter but stock too low.
Stock stuck in tight range; successor announcement to CEO Bob Iger could break logjam and provide catalyst.
Similar industrial pipes and valves exposure; down six consecutive points but Cramer prefers this over other equipment options.

Chili's CEO Kevin Hawk delivering blowout turnaround: Chili's same-store sales +8.6% vs. 6.1% expected; 25-cent earnings beat; two-year comp over 40%; 19 consecutive quarters of growth; menu simplification cuts costs; value leadership with $3-4 cheaper per-person check vs. competitors; quality upgrades (50% more bacon, domestic ribs) driving volume. Stock down 14% over 12 months despite triple since start 2024; now poised to burst higher.
Correct nuclear play; ahead of everybody else in nuclear technology and capacity.
Analog chipmaker play similar to Roper of old (pipes and valves); had great quarter and preferred over other options in sector.

Best stock in the business by Cramer's count; many wrote off as worst Magnificent Seven but Gemini proved superior; owns YouTube, Waymo self-driving, Google Search—multiple competitive moats; recent buyback into Charitable Trust.
Thought to be terrific and part of solution, not problem, in healthcare sector; positioned as one of better healthcare stocks.
Stock driven by GLP-1 clinical trial reports, not earnings; potential for new data announcement to get stock rolling.

CEO Bob Jordan turnaround complete with Elliott Management backing; new revenue initiatives (assigned seats, bag fees, leg-room) working; guidance implies EPS can more than quadruple this year; stock up 19% post-earnings.
CEO Bob Jordan + Elliott Management activist driving transformation; new fee-based model working with strong forward guidance; EPS expected to quadruple in 2026.
Down 14% despite top/bottom beat; Macau margins disappointing from high promotional costs; gambling stocks all struggling; too hard to game given Chinese consumer dependency and CCP crackdown risk.

Down 14% despite beat on top and bottom line; Macau margins disappointed from higher promotional costs on NBA preseason; too risky given CCP crackdown risk on conspicuous consumption; Chinese consumer volatility makes all gambling plays uncertain.
Drug distributor middleman targeted by president's healthcare crackdown; traditionally flies on earnings as doctors/pharma execs wink at McKesson as culprit for high healthcare costs.
Real wins from bountiful acquisition spree; CEO can spread wealth beyond current heavy Kruda exposure.
Buy despite massive earnings slashing and current yield 6.75%; very well-run company; stock so low worth buying; Rob Cats does good job.
CEO Jensen Wong only one who saw chip shortage coming and stocked accordingly; visionary leadership on AI and data-center capacity.
Snack division struggling due to GLP-1 weight-loss drugs; CEO Raymon Magguire may take serious actions to reduce snack dependence; stock up $5 today on sector recovery.
Terrific yield but no new blockbuster in sight to move the needle; needs catalyst beyond dividend sustainability.
Software consulting company expecting terrific quarter; CEO Alex Karp is a fighter; stock will reignite after dormant period.
Volatile 20-point moves on any commentary; expected to be up this time but highly speculative.

Enterprise software business suffering valuation collapse; acquisition strategy of buying software companies no longer in favor; Q4 revenue miss plus full-year guidance miss; won't recover until software cohort exits penalty box.

Mixed quarter with revenue miss paired to small earnings beat; full-year guidance missed on every line; current quarter guidance short; enterprise software cohort out of fashion (valuations compressed like Service Now, Adobe, Salesforce); unlikely to bounce until software gets respect again.

Food distributor to restaurants; modest top/bottom beat impressive given industry headwinds; January strong start; guidance raised to high end; trading 18x earnings with 2.6% yield; positive volume growth territory.

Shot up nearly 11% on Tuesday on modest beat; January strong start rare for restaurant industry; expects FY2026 earnings at high end of guidance; CEO Kevin Hurricane confirms positive volume growth and momentum.
HVAC company benefiting from data-center cooling demand; beat Q4 with strong bookings; 12-14% YoY earnings growth guidance for 2026; residential headwind offset by commercial strength.
Rallied 8% on strong Q4 beat and excellent booking numbers; issued bullish 2026 forecast with 12-14% EPS growth; data center strength offsetting residential weakness.

Advertising-tech chronic underperformer; down 68% in 2026, down another 20% YTD; CFO fired after 5 months (major red flag); valuation collapsed from 62x to 15x but confidence lacking until turnaround evident.

Chronic underperformer; down 68% in 2026 YTD already worst performer on S&P 500 last year; CFO fired after only 5 months major red flag; struggling to adapt to AI era; Wall Street fears ad-tech middlemen obsolete as Meta handles own AI targeting; down another 17% this week; now 15x forward earnings vs. 62x last year.

Analog chip maker issued tremendous Q1 guidance despite modest Q4; leveraged to industrial/auto recovery; data-center business up 64% YoY; sequential earnings growth reverses typical seasonal decline—first positive turn since 1985.

Tremendous guidance on Tuesday night; stock rallied 10% next day; analog chip maker with 9% data center exposure; much more leveraged to real-world economy; data center business up 64% YoY; first profitable quarter since 1985.

Equipment rental company delivered miss on revenue/earnings; full-year guidance light; mega-projects favor large construction companies; down 13%; stock cheap at <17x but may remain in purgatory until construction recovery visible.

Plunged 13% on weak results; revenue miss and earnings miss; FY guidance light across all metrics; mega-projects for large companies compress margins; stock cheap at 17x forward earnings with $5B buyback but may remain in purgatory until construction recovery confirmed.
Not the right way to play nuclear power; prefer EVE (Brookfield's Westinghouse unit) which leads nuclear development.
Great earnings this week yet stock still declined—analyst meeting critical to understand disconnect; storage company now part of problem, not solution, for customers like Apple.
